response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with genuine systems chops (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second, strong testing culture, open-source leadership), but he misses the role's hard requirements by a wide margin. Documented C++ experience totals roughly one year (a 7-month stint at National Instruments plus a thesis project) against a required 8 years, C++ is absent from his own skills list, and his recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js product work rather than cloud compute infrastructure. He also lacks formal technical-leadership experience leading teams or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers at the level this role demands.
strengths: Real high-scale systems experience: low-latency, async I/O microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus authored test frameworks and ran workshops and coding dojos
Broad, adaptable technical range across backend, infrastructure (Kubernetes, AWS CDK, Docker), and open-source project ownership (GISCollective)
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ requirement (~1 year documented; C++ not even listed in his skills section)
No formal technical-lead or people-leadership role; mentoring is limited to workshops and community teaching rather than leading engineering teams
Recent experience is web/product-focused (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than cloud compute platforms or large-scale infrastructure strategy

key_factors.factor: Lack of technical leadership track record
